data.title: Figma design collaboration: Pull via REST API and manifest merge
data.description:
## Context

The Push epic establishes a Figma collaboration loop where designers revise DSO's wireframes and provide a revision image. The implementation agent must interpret visual changes from an image, which is lossy. This epic adds structured pull-back: DSO reads the revised design from Figma as a complete JSON node tree via the REST API, maps it to the spatial-layout.json schema, and regenerates the full 3-artifact manifest by merging visual changes with preserved behavioral specifications.

## Success Criteria

1. User provides Figma URL; file key extracted and stored on ticket; PAT auth via dso-config.conf (design.figma_pat with env-var fallback FIGMA_PAT)
2. User-triggered re-sync reads Figma via REST API (GET /v1/files/:key), maps JSON node properties to spatial-layout.json schema, regenerates complete 3-artifact manifest — visual changes update positions/sizes/styles/text while behavioral specs (interactions, states, a11y, responsive) are preserved unchanged
3. Designer-added components created as tag: NEW with designer_added: true and behavioral_spec_status: INCOMPLETE; removed components cleaned from all 3 artifacts with warning if behavioral_spec_status was COMPLETE; ID-linkage validated (every ID in spatial-layout.json exists in wireframe.svg and tokens.md)
4. Re-sync acquires file lock (TTL-based, stale-lock cleanup), presents change summary for user confirmation, supports --non-interactive flag for CI
5. Successful re-sync records sync metadata on ticket: Figma file key, timestamp, components added/modified/removed counts, behavioral specs preserved count

## Scenario Analysis

16 surviving scenarios. Key risks: PAT in config exposed in VCS (mitigation: env-var fallback), stale lock on crash (mitigation: TTL), behavioral spec silently deleted on removal (mitigation: warn on COMPLETE specs), ID-linkage rename collisions (mitigation: orphan detection), large file OOM (mitigation: depth-limited traversal).

## Dependencies

- Push epic (148e-61c8)
- Figma PAT with file_content:read scope
- Any paid Figma plan with Dev or Full seat

## Approach

REST API with PAT authentication — stable, production-grade, fully headless. Merge algorithm preserves behavioral specs while updating visual properties from Figma JSON. No MCP, no OAuth, no beta APIs. File locking prevents concurrent corruption.

### Planning Intelligence Log

- **Web research**: triggered (shared with Push epic research)
- **Scenario analysis**: triggered (18 red team → 16 surviving blue team)
